response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and DLang developer but lacks nearly every must-have for this embedded networking security role. There is no native Japanese proficiency listed (only English/Romanian/German basic), no Linux kernel modification experience, minimal recent C programming (one ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015), and no exposure to LAN-switch firmware, MACsec, 802.1X, switch chip SDKs, or L2/L3 networking protocols. The profile is web/cloud/GIS-oriented, not embedded security.
strengths: Long, varied software engineering career with strong testing/TDD discipline
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI
Comfortable with Linux environments, Docker, and CI/CD tooling
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
No demonstrated Linux kernel source-code modification or embedded firmware experience
No security/networking domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, switch chips, L2/L3)
